cardiometabolic_tracker — v0.3.0

Export / printable summary · Deployed 2026-06-11 to pxbuilder-aomrani.canvasmedical.com · build commit c940f8e (on 7b931b0 v0.2.5 baseline)
pytest: 150 collected · 0 skipped · 150 mocked · 0 live live browser (Tier 0/2 targeted): 15/15 one login cycle, screenshots in-session zero new network calls from the iframe
Per Gate 4: the pytest suite mocks the Canvas SDK and cannot catch runtime behavior; the 15 live checks (chart render, button visibility on valid vs validation-blocked patients, print-media state, disclosure text, console triage) are the runtime gate for this release.

Highlights

  • One-click clinician-shareable summary. An Export button in the chart header opens the browser print dialog; saving as PDF yields a single-page GLP-1 progress summary (patient name, DOB, export date, plugin version, the chart in its final state, a stats block, and the three trial citations).
  • Stats block maps the existing payload only — baseline and latest weight with dates, total %TBWL, 5/10/15% milestone status with first-crossing dates, 4-week velocity with flag badges, and the active agent with its matched trial band. No recomputation, no second data path.
  • Uncertainty disclosure survives export verbatim. For liraglutide patients the band prints as “SCALE, ±1 SD” and the full estimated-bounds disclosure (published mean ±1 SD, LOCF, responder rates) is reproduced in the stats block, verified live on P4.
  • Validation-blocked patients cannot export: the error view is a separate template that never contains the button (verified live on the no-data patient).
  • Mechanism decision: client-side PDF libraries and the SDK’s GenerateFullChartPDFEffect were evaluated and rejected (details in Observations); the print pipeline ships with zero new dependencies.

Screenshots

Bugs found & fixed

  • No plugin defects surfaced during this build. pytest stayed green through implementation (137 → 150) and all live checks passed on the first fully-triaged run.
  • Smoke-harness fix: the new Tier 0 script initially used an input[type=submit] login selector; the Canvas login page needs button:has-text("Login") (matching the proven tier2 script).
  • Console-triage hardening: raw console assertion failed on host-class noise — a host S3 background image (Background@2x.jpg) intermittently resets, plus CSP report-only notices. Added URL attribution (page.on('requestfailed')) and classified per RUNBOOK §5; zero plugin-attributable errors remained.

Observations & architectural findings

  • window.print() is available inside the about:srcdoc iframe (verified live, typeof === 'function') and prints only the iframe document — the print pipeline is viable for plugin views.
  • CDN scripts load fine in the iframe (d3 has shipped from jsdelivr since v0.1), so CSP was not the blocker for a PDF library; the blockers were the one-CDN-script constraint (a true PDF needs jsPDF + an SVG renderer = two) and unverifiable allow-downloads sandbox behavior.
  • The SDK has a native GenerateFullChartPDFEffect — but it renders the entire native chart, asynchronously (task list, ~10 min), and creates EHR artifacts; unusable for a plugin-view export. Documented capability worth knowing.
  • Print-media emulation (page.emulateMedia) applies to iframes, enabling automated print-preview assertions without opening a dialog.
  • RUNBOOK staleness found during the cold-start stress test (full gap report committed alongside): current version said v0.2.1 (actual 0.2.5→now 0.3.0), pytest count said 107 (actual 137→150), and the no-data patient is Jane Will, not “Carol Singh”.

Future version considerations

  • Real print-dialog pass on the Desktop session — automation verifies print-media CSS and window.print availability, but a human print-preview + save-as-PDF on the reviewer’s Chrome closes the loop (this report is that request).
  • Predominant-display-unit selection (carried from v0.2.5 review finding 2) — required before any deployment with kg-recording patients; the export inherits whatever DISPLAY_UNIT ships.
  • Unicode-minus alignment (v0.2.5 finding 5) — fold into any future style pass.
  • Reference-file cleanup for clinician review: glp1_science_reference.md’s PROGRAM_TEMPLATES dict still carries 56: 8.4 for liraglutide %TBWL (kg figure in a percent field — remnant of the corrected mislabel). The plugin does not consume that dict; flagged, not shipped.
  • Out of scope by design: server-side PDF, emailing, document persistence, batch export, export customization.

Tooling & skill notes

  • tier0_v03_export.js reuses the tier2 single-login pattern and adds print-media assertions + failing-request attribution; the attribution listener is worth backporting to tier2_v02_assertions.js.
  • The v0.2.5 review’s P8 screenshot gap is closed in this run (P8 re-render captured in the same login cycle).
  • Stray-conversion-literal test now also scans chart.html and the bare 2.2  token (review finding 3 — one-line hardening landed with the v0.3.0 tests).
